programme.title: Royal Births, Marriages & Deaths
programme.desc: This time: Prince William's wedding to Catherine Middleton attracted a global audience of over one billion viewers, none so keen as the craftswomen of the Royal School of Needlework worked on the wedding dress and veil fabric for months but had never seen the finished product until the big day; rebel princess Princess Margaret, who broke royal protocol, insisting on cremation rather than burial so that she could be laid to rest with her beloved father; the story of George II, the monarch who proved that Elvis was not the only king to die on the toilet; and the stifling conditions in the birthing chamber of Jane Seymour who died just 12 days after giving birth to Edward VI. Exec Prod: Oliver Wright; Series Dir: Anna Cox; Coming Up Roses (S1 Ep6)
Stunning series telling the story of 1000 years of monarchy via powerful, pivotal moments.

programme.title: Beatrice and Eugenie: A Royal Dilemma
programme.desc: Granddaughters of Queen Elizabeth II, daughters of Andrew and Sarah, Princesses Beatrice and Eugenie have weathered many controversies to befall their parents, as well as being personally mocked for everything from their clothes to their privilege. Now Andrew faces the ultimate humiliation of losing his royal titles, status and residence, what does the future hold for the 9th and 12th in line to the throne?

programme.title: Fergie and the Fake Sheikh Scandal
programme.desc: This programme relives the breathtaking story of one of the most shocking falls from grace in modern royal history and the undercover sting that changed Sarah Ferguson's life forever. In 2010, Fergie was secretly filmed by undercover reporter Mazher Mahmood the infamous 'Fake Sheikh', apparently offering to sell access to her ex-husband, Prince Andrew, for £500,000. The explosive footage, published by the News of the World, sent shockwaves around the world, dragging the monarchy into scandal and leaving Fergie publicly disgraced. This film traces the full, extraordinary journey from fairy-tale royal wedding to tabloid target, and from global celebrity to financial ruin. Using dramatic archive, sting footage, and candid interviews with former News of the World journalists, royal experts and insiders, it reveals how Fergie's spending spiralled out of control, how desperation clouded her judgement, and how she walked straight into the Fake Sheikh's trap. Exec Prod: Nicola Lloyd; Series Prod: Simon Harries; Naked TV

programme.title: Most Shocking Celebrity Moments

programme.desc: Paramedics take Shaun, 53, to resus after he collapses at home following multiple seizures. Time is of the essence to determine what might be triggering his seizures. Harriet, eight, has a suspected broken toe. If an x-ray reveals that her toe is dislocated, it will need to be manipulated back into position. Paramedics bring in 94-year-old Margaret, who has a high heart rate and dangerously low oxygen levels. And 79-year-old Tommy, with a history of heart attacks, arrives. He's all too familiar with Barnsley Casualty having been in numerous times, so volunteer Jane pops in to see him. Series Prod: Kayleigh Smith; Prods/Dirs: Philip Hirst/Matt Lees/Anna Evans; Series Dir: Joe Lister; Exec Prods: Kerry Brierley/Elaine Hackett; Crackit Productions Limited (S11 Ep3)

programme.title: Surgeons: A Matter of Life or Death
programme.desc: Seventeen-year-old Alfie's life is slowly being destroyed by an incredibly rare tumour growing at speed into his nose and cheek. Alfie must have surgery - and fast - but it's a deeply complex process. Surgeon Shaz Ahmed must delicately attempt to free up the mass, before pushing the tumour back down Alfie's airway and out through his mouth. Elizabeth's kidneys have grown to the size of two watermelons and are threatening to swamp her vital organs. Surgeon Mr Bhatt must remove both of them before her health deteriorates any further. Finally, after radiotherapy to treat skin cancer on his forehead, Simon has been left with a significant area where the skull is crumbling. He is now at risk of a potentially life-threatening infection and neurosurgeon David Davies and plastic surgeon Marco Malahias believe there is not a moment to lose. Series Prods: Sue Learoyd/Lizzie Faulkner; Prods/Dirs: Luke Farrell/Alex Gower-Jackson; Exec Prod: Malcolm Brinkworth; Brinkworth Productions Ltd (S3 Ep3/6)
Remarkable life-saving operations and extraordinary stories of surgical skill.

programme.title: Will You Get Dementia?
programme.desc: We are more scared of a diagnosis of dementia than we are of cancer. NHS GP Dr Claire Taylor meets patients living with dementia, and investigates what we can do to help prevent it developing.the condition

programme.title: Spain with Michael Portillo
programme.desc: Michael travels to the northern Basque region, home to two of Spain's most exciting cities - Bilbao and San Sebastian. He celebrates the extraordinary Guggenheim Museum, before joining locals for pintxos, the Basque finger food which he's told should not be confused with tapas! Michael then heads along the coast towards San Sebastián, stopping en route at the Basque rural games, where feats of sporting skill and endurance based on past times like farming and fishing are tested. Arriving in San Sebastián, Michael meets Osane, who takes him on a walking tour, the best way to see the city. They delve into the city's history on its oldest surviving street and reveal how it burned to the ground during Napoleon's Peninsular War, leading to its rebirth as the holiday resort of the Spanish royal family. Prod/Dir: Dave Minchin; Exec Prod: Charlie Bunce; Curve Media Limited (Ep3)
Join Michael on a journey of discovery deep into his secret Spain off the tourist trail.

programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programme.desc: Ben ventures into the forested wilderness of Scandinavia to stay with two remarkable artists: Tronde, a 69-year-old Norwegian, and Robert, his 61-year-old Lebanese best friend and business partner. The pair relocated to the Norwegian wilds later in life, and despite both facing serious health challenges, they are more determined than ever to expand and embrace their forest hideaway. Ben throws himself into their world of art and nature, tackling the physical tasks the pair now struggle with. He sleeps in a whimsical 'doll's house', steps inside the inner sanctum of their art studio, dyes wool, and helps construct a human-sized artistic ant's nest. He also swims in the nearby lake, where Tronde shares the story of a family tragedy. Along the way, Ben gains a glimpse into the region's rich cultural heritage, and the friends reveal how they open their home to guests-inviting others to experience their art, their hospitality, and, most memorably, their homemade waffles. Exec Prod: Natalie Wilkinson; Dir: Natalie Wilkinson; Rushlight Films (S21 Ep2)
Ben Fogle travels round the world to visit people who have given up the rat race.
